description: Proposal 0029: a capability result is validated against descriptor.output_schema post-execution. This KAT exercises the shared subset all three reference impls agree on — the required-keys dimension (a missing required key is a violation; Python's full jsonschema, the TS host's minimal check, and verify.mjs all agree). `valid:false` means the result violates the schema, so a strict/require call denies output_schema_validation_failed while the default warns.

cases.note: conforming: extra keys are fine when only required is asserted
violation: multiple required keys, one missing
conforming: multiple required keys all present
violation: non-object result satisfies no required key
no required constraint: any result conforms
